title: Special Methods & Next Steps 🌟
description: Magic methods and your Python journey ahead.

slides.title: Magic/Dunder Methods ✨
slides.content:
# Special Methods

**Dunder methods** (double underscore) let you customize how objects behave with Python's built-in operations:

| Method | Purpose | Triggered by |
|--------|---------|--------------|
| `__init__` | Constructor | `MyClass()` |
| `__str__` | String representation | `print(obj)` |
| `__repr__` | Developer representation | `repr(obj)` |
| `__len__` | Length | `len(obj)` |
| `__eq__` | Equality | `obj1 == obj2` |
| `__lt__` | Less than | `obj1 < obj2` |
| `__add__` | Addition | `obj1 + obj2` |

slides.title: Magic Methods Demo ✨
slides.content: Make your objects work with print() and ==:

quizData.question: What does __str__ customize?
quizData.explanation: __str__ defines what print() shows for your object.

slides.title: Your Python Journey 🚀
slides.content:
# What's Next?

Congratulations! 🎉 You've learned Python's core foundations:

✅ Variables, types, operators
✅ Control flow (if/else, loops)
✅ Functions and scope
✅ Data structures (lists, dicts, sets, tuples)
✅ String methods
✅ File I/O and error handling
✅ OOP basics (classes, inheritance)

**Where to go next:**
* 📦 **Modules & Packages** — import and use libraries
* 🌐 **Web Development** — Flask, Django
* 📊 **Data Science** — NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib
* 🤖 **Machine Learning** — scikit-learn, TensorFlow
* 🎮 **Game Dev** — Pygame

Keep coding, keep learning! 🐍✨

quizData.question: What are dunder methods?

options.text: Methods that start and end with double underscores

quizData.explanation: Dunder (double underscore) methods like __init__, __str__ have special meaning in Python.
